preflight_summary.scope: RAG assistant that answers internal engineering questions from wiki pages and runbooks.
preflight_summary.system_risk_profile: RAG; internal; confidential data; no external actions
important_fixes: Add poisoning and cross-tenant retrieval tests.
non_blocking_backlog: Add retrieval freshness scoring after launch.

facts: Retrieval source inventory exists at docs/retrieval-sources.md.
Data classification notes mention internal and confidential content.
assumptions: The vector database is shared across multiple teams.
missing_architecture_evidence: No proof that document-level authorization is enforced before prompt assembly.

narrative: What are we working on: an internal RAG assistant over engineering documents.
What can go wrong: poisoned or unauthorized documents can shape model output.
What are we going to do: require retrieval authorization, poisoning tests, and provenance evidence.
Did we do a good job: Security should validate source ownership and test output before rollout.

threats.title: Indirect prompt injection through retrieved content
threats.attack_path: A wiki page contains hidden instructions that are retrieved and inserted into the answer prompt.
threats.likely_impact: The assistant may disclose internal information or produce unsafe guidance.
gaps: No indirect prompt injection test result was found
No source quarantine process was found
threats.mitigation_decision: Require poisoning tests and untrusted-content labeling before Security review.
threats.residual_risk: medium if tests pass and source quarantine is documented

threats.title: Unauthorized cross-team retrieval
threats.attack_path: A user asks a question that retrieves confidential chunks from a team they cannot access directly.
threats.likely_impact: Confidential engineering information may be exposed across authorization boundaries.
threats.mitigation_decision: Block release until retrieval uses end-user authorization at query and context assembly time.
threats.residual_risk: low to medium after authorization tests pass
threats.stride_translation: Information disclosure and elevation of privilege

evidence_gaps.title: Indirect prompt injection test results
evidence_gaps.requested_artifact: Attach tests showing hostile retrieved instructions are labeled untrusted and cannot override system instructions.

evidence_gaps.title: Retrieval authorization tests
evidence_gaps.requested_artifact: Attach tests proving retrieval respects user, team, tenant, and document-level authorization.

security_tests.title: Poisoned retrieved document test
security_tests.objective: Verify hostile retrieved content cannot control instructions or output policy.
steps: Insert a document containing hidden instructions
Ask a question that retrieves the document
security_tests.expected_secure_behavior: The assistant treats retrieved instructions as untrusted content and refuses any attempt to override policy.
security_tests.evidence_to_retain: Source document, retrieved chunk trace, generated answer, and pass/fail assertion.

security_tests.title: Cross-team retrieval authorization test
security_tests.objective: Verify document-level access controls apply before retrieval and before prompt assembly.
prerequisites: Two users with different document access
steps: Index a document visible to only one user
security_tests.expected_secure_behavior: No unauthorized chunks are retrieved, assembled into prompts, emitted, or logged in readable form.
security_tests.evidence_to_retain: Access fixtures, retrieval trace, prompt assembly trace, and response transcript.

engineering_backlog.title: Add RAG poisoning regression coverage
engineering_backlog.problem: Retrieved content can carry adversarial instructions into the model context.
engineering_backlog.recommended_change: Add poisoned-document fixtures, untrusted-content labeling, and assertions that retrieved instructions cannot override system policy.
acceptance_criteria: Poisoned document test runs in CI
Logs show retrieved content boundary labels

engineering_backlog.title: Enforce end-user authorization in retrieval
engineering_backlog.problem: The current evidence does not prove retrieval respects the caller's document access.
engineering_backlog.recommended_change: Pass end-user authorization context through retrieval and context assembly, with deny-by-default behavior.

unresolved_decisions: Whether the shared vector database is acceptable for confidential documents.
human_review_points: Security should review retrieval authorization and poisoning test results.
